description_long: This sculpture depicts the head of a serene figure, likely inspired by East Asian Buddhist iconography, carved from a pale stone material. The face is calm, with closed eyes, a gentle smile, and detailed features such as a headdress and elongated earlobes. The back of the head is roughly hewn, contrasting with the smooth, detailed front and sides. The sculpture is mounted on a rectangular stone pedestal with a stepped base, which adds to its monumentality. Weathering and discoloration are visible, especially on the upper and rear surfaces, suggesting outdoor exposure. A small plaque is affixed to the front of the pedestal, though its text is not legible in the images.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of a serene, headdressed head on a rectangular pedestal, with detailed facial features and a rough, unfinished back.
description_view_0: Frontal view showing the calm face, headdress, and the plaque on the pedestal.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile, elongated earlobe, and the depth of the headdress.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rough, uncarved back of the head and pedestal.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile, headdress details, and the textured surface.
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is mounted on a stone pedestal, suggesting an outdoor or public installation, possibly in a park or plaza.
